title: Positron: um ambiente de desenvolvimento integrado (IDE)
description:
O que esperar desse curso?

Positron é uma IDE moderna criada pela Posit para oferecer uma experiência integrada em ciência de dados, unificando notebooks, scripts e consoles em um único ambiente. Com suporte nativo para R e Python, ele facilita a escrita de código, a exploração de dados, a criação de gráficos e a execução de análises de forma fluida, sem a necessidade de alternar entre diferentes ferramentas.

Construído sobre a base do Code OSS, o Positron mantém a familiaridade de editores populares, mas adiciona recursos específicos para análise de dados, como visualização de data frames, painel de plots, integração com Quarto e suporte a aplicações como Streamlit. É gratuito, extensível e pensado para dar aos cientistas de dados uma experiência coesa e intuitiva.

ELA É A NOVA QUERIDINHA!

Vamos aprender e futricar essa nova IDE juntes?

#VemAí #Positron

title: ✨When LLMs guess, tools know: tool calling in R with ellmer ✨
description:
R-Ladies Cologne are hosting another event! We are excited to have Veerle Eeftink - van Leemput with us, who will share her insights into the world of LLMs 🚀

**✨When LLMs guess, tools know: tool calling in R with ellmer ✨**
Large language models are great at sounding confident… and occasionally great at being wrong. Counting things? Doing some calculations? Analysing data? Not their finest moments. In this talk, we’ll look at how R, ellmer, and a bit of tool calling can turn “educated guesses” into real, reliable, reproducible results. We’ll break down what tool calling actually is, why it matters, and how to use it to make LLMs far more useful in real applications. Expect practical examples, a peek behind the curtain of how these models work, and a few tips and tricks to work successfully with LLMs!

If you’re up for joining us, RSVP here to get the access.

To stay up-to-date with our future events, make sure to also follow us on [Bluesky](https://bsky.app/profile/rladies-cologne.bsky.social), [mastodon](https://fosstodon.org/@rladies_cologne) and [linkedin](https://www.linkedin.com/company/r-ladies-cologne/).

title: From Basics to Advanced Health Analytics: Exploring Diabetes Data
description:
In this session, we will walk through a full end-to-end workflow of health data analysis in R using a simulated database based of diabetes.

We will begin with **exploratory data analysis (EDA)** to identify initial patterns, distributions, and data quality issues. Then, we’ll apply data **wrangling** techniques to prepare the dataset for robust statistical investigation. Building on this foundation, we will create effective and **elegant visualizations** with ggplot2 to communicate insights clearly and intuitively.

Next, we will introduce **inferential modeling** for qualitative outcomes, discussing how to estimate associations, interpret the results, and translate findings into meaningful epidemiological insights.

Finally, we will explore **cluster analysis** to reveal underlying subgroups within the population and highlight the heterogeneity of diabetes profiles.

The session is designed to transition smoothly from basic to more advanced methods, offering a practical and reproducible framework for health data analysis.

No previous experience with medical datasets is required—all code will be shown step by step and shared afterwards.

title: 16º Meet Up: Pré-processamento e Tratamento de Dados com Tidyverse em R
description: Este minicurso apresenta a utilização do Tidyverse, um dos principais conjuntos de pacotes do R para ciência de dados. Com exemplos aplicados, os participantes irão percorrer um fluxo completo de pré-processamento de dados, iniciando pela importação de arquivos em diferentes formatos e avançando para etapas essenciais de manipulação: verificação e tratamento de valores ausentes (NAs), conferência e ajuste dos tipos de variáveis, correção de inconsistências, renomeação e criação de novas variáveis, seleção de colunas, filtragem de linhas e junção de bases. O minicurso também abordará análises descritivas e análises de correlação, demonstrando como o Tidyverse pode facilitar a exploração e organização dos dados de forma eficiente e reproduzível.

title: Lunch & learn with Casey Aguilar-Gervase: Design of Everyday Shiny Apps
description:
Donald Norman’s *Design of Everyday Things* shows how even smart people struggle with bad design--flipping the wrong switch or pushing instead of pulling. Shiny apps have the same problem when function outweighs experience. As developers, we’re the door designers, and it’s our job to make tools people actually enjoy using.
Good news: you don’t need an art degree to do this. I’ll walk through practical design principles--composition, color, scale, and proportion--and how to apply them directly in Shiny code. With real examples and actionable patterns, you’ll learn how good design becomes not a luxury, but a necessity.

title: tidywater (water chemistry) and building packages using base R
description:
Hello Denver R Ladies!

Please join us on November 4th for a cozy evening of R talks showcasing the awesome work of R users in Denver and surrounding areas.

Doors open at 6:30 p.m., presentations start at 7:00 p.m. We hope to see you there!

This event is co-hosted by Denver R Ladies and Denver R Users.

**Introduction to the tidywater package**
Libby McKenna and Sierra Johnson - Brown and Caldwell

Our package (available on CRAN), tidywater, consolidates public models for water treatment modeling. This package was developed to address the need for a common modeling platform for our industry, drinking water engineering, which previously required assembling results from multiple tools. Additionally, an R Shiny webapp was developed for people who want to access the models, but don't have the time to learn R.
Libby recently presented at posit::conf 2025 in Atlanta

**I'll see you data.table and tidyverse and raise you base R**
Peter DeWitt - CU Anshutz/ NREL

The tools for data manipulation provided by the tidyverse's dplyr and tidyr are extremely useful, as are dcast, melt, and the set\* methods for data.table. However, there is an argument to be made that base R, in some cases, can be preferable to either of these popular R dialects. In this presentation we will explore a specific example where base R methods can be preferable and how to build R packages to support different dialects without dependencies or imports.

title: Workshop - Introduction to R (Part 2)
description:
We're back with Part 2 of our Introduction to R workshop series. While this workshop builds on the previous one, **new participants are also welcome to join us!** We will provide the materials from Part 1 for you to catch up.

We welcome all genders and identities to attend our events at R-Ladies Vienna.

**ℹ️ Abstract**
**R** is a powerful programming language and environment widely used across fields from statistics and economics to psychology, biology, and more. It provides a versatile analytical toolkit for anyone working with data.

The goal of this workshop series is for our attendees to become acquainted with R to start their data analytics journey. In this follow-up session, we will focus on:

* Data processing with the dplyr package
* Data visualization with the ggplot2 package
* Loops, functions, and conditional statements

**📊 Bring your own data!**
If you have a dataset you would like to analyze and visualize during the workshop, we encourage you to bring it along and apply the techniques to your data of interest.

**💡 What to prepare**

* Before the workshop, install **R** and **RStudio** on your laptop by following the 2-step guide at **[https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)**
* On the workshop day, bring your laptop to follow the workshop material closely and code along with us!
* (Optional) Bring a dataset you would like to work with
* (Optional) If you're new to the series, we will send you the link to the Part 1 materials upon registration. Take your time to review the topics we covered in Part 1.
(Basic data types and structures, reading data into R and exporting from R, basic data processing and visualization with base R)

**⏱️ Duration**

title: Aprendizado causal: um guia inicial
description:
O que esperar desse curso?

Este minicurso é uma introdução prática à inferência causal, apresentando desde os conceitos fundamentais até técnicas básicas de descoberta causal. Iniciaremos explorando redes bayesianas e grafos causais e aprendendo a representar relações de dependência entre variáveis.

Em seguida, abordaremos modelos de estrutura causal e o conceito de d-separação, entendendo como identificar independências condicionais e determinar quais variáveis são relevantes para inferências causais. Por fim, apresentaremos os princípios do causal discovery, oferecendo uma visão inicial de como descobrir relações causais a partir de dados observacionais.

Ao longo do minicurso, os participantes desenvolverão habilidades práticas e intuitivas para interpretar e analisar dados com foco em causalidade, tornando este conteúdo acessível para iniciantes e aplicável em diferentes contextos de pesquisa e análise de dados.

title: Custom city maps in R
description:
Want to learn about geospatial analysis in R? Are your walls looking a little bare? Or maybe you’re searching for a unique, data-inspired gift for friends or family?
Come join R-Ladies Ottawa for a hands-on workshop where you’ll learn how to make minimalist custom city maps using open-source tools!

**🧐 What you'll learn**
In this workshop, we’ll cover:

* The fundamentals of geospatial analysis (e.g., points, lines, polygons, projections)
* How to access and work with OpenStreetMap data
* How to use R packages like osmdata and ggplot2 to create your own beautiful maps
* How to incorporate data from activity tracking apps such as Strava or Runkeeper to visualize your favourite running or cycling routes (time-permitting)

**🧰 Before the workshop**
To get the most out of this session, please:

* Install R and RStudio (or another IDE such as Positron) on your personal computer

title: #stayrelevant: how to keep up with changes in the R ecosystem
description:
Hi R-Ladies SB!

Have you ever returned to an old project only to realize that…

* Your code no longer runs
* Your code runs, but you get a warning about a deprecated function
* A more efficient or modern approach exists
* There’s an exciting new feature or tool, but your version of R isn’t compatible

We were curious about best practices for staying on top of new package releases, software updates, and emerging tools (e.g. RStudio → Positron, RMarkdown → Quarto), so we asked around, only to discover that many of our colleagues were wondering the same thing. So, we decided to turn that curiosity into a community conversation!

**Join us for an open discussion where we’ll** **swap strategies, share challenges, and collectively brainstorm how to #stayrelevant** in the ever-evolving world of R and data science. To make the most of this shared learning, we’d love to turn our discussion into a **collaborative blog post**. All participants will be invited to join as co-authors.

Please bring your laptop so we can work collaboratively in a shared Google Doc, and come ready to share an example of a time you discovered a new data science tool or approach (and how you first heard about it).

As always, all R-users, from beginners to experts, are welcome!

\~Your R-Ladies SB Co-organizers

title: R-Ladies at R Dev Days with Dr Heather Turner
description:
Rladies Melbourne will be hosting Dr Heather Turner who is a leading R developer and statistician with over two decades of experience.

**R-Ladies at R Dev Days**
Under my research software engineering fellowship "Sustainability and EDI (Equality, Diversity, and Inclusion) in the R Project" I have led the organisation of nine "R Dev Day"s, where R users work collaboratively on contributions to base R and to infrastructure that supports such contribution. Around 150 people have participated in these events, with good representation from groups that have historically been underrepresented in the contributor community, including women and gender minorities.

In this talk, I'll give some insight into the tasks that people have worked on at R Dev Days, highlighting some examples that R-Ladies have been involved in. It's broader than you might think, including translation, dashboards, websites, the contributor guide, and CRAN utilities, as well as the code and documentation of base R. I'll explain different ways to get involved, including joining the upcoming hybrid events at Monash University (https://pretix.eu/r-contributors/r-dev-day-oz-25/) and the University of Auckland (https://pretix.eu/r-contributors/r-dev-day-nz-25/).

**R Dev Day @ Australia 2025 - November 21**
Dr Turner has additionally organised R Dev Day @ Australia 2025. This is an open, collaborative event for people interested in contributing to the code and documentation of base R, or to infrastructure that supports such contribution. Both new and experienced contributors are welcome!

You can choose to join in-person at Monash City Campus, Melbourne, or online.

[Registration](https://pretix.eu/r-contributors/r-dev-day-oz-25/) will remain open until **Sunday November 16, 2025**.

***Register and stay tuned for more details on her presentation and the location for the 12th of November.***

title: Developing your data visualisation skills in ggplot2
description: In this session, we'll be working through the process of developing a visualisation in ggplot2. We'll start with a brand new dataset and create some exploratory plots; sketch out some ideas and decide what we want to communicate; build a basic plot in ggplot2; and then refine our chart to create something that's artistic and aesthetic, yet effective. Some basic knowledge of the tidyverse will be helpful, but all of the code in this session will be explained and made available afterwards.

title: Cómo ejecutar y personalizar un LLM para tu propio código.
description:
Titulo: Cómo ejecutar y personalizar un LLM (Modelo de Lenguaje de Gran Tamaño) para tu propio código. Un ejemplo usando Python, React, Postgres y Docker.

En este meetup **híbrido** de la mano de Kristin tendremos una sesión práctica, donde instalaremos y configuraremos un LLM local en nuestro equipo. Aprenderás a personalizarlo para comprender tus datos y adaptar sus respuestas a tu flujo de trabajo. También exploraremos cómo integrarlo en aplicaciones como Freyoto, una herramienta de consulta de fotos. Juntos, analizaremos desafíos reales de análisis y exploraremos cómo la combinación de LLM con herramientas de software puede generar información más rápidamente.

Title: How to run and customize an LLM (Large Language Model) for your own code with an example using Python, React, Postgres, and Docker.
In this hands-on session, we’ll install and configure a local LLM on your own machine. You’ll learn how to customize it to understand your data and tailor its responses to your workflow. We’ll also explore how to integrate it into applications like Freyoto, a photo-querying tool. Together, we’ll discuss real analysis challenges and explore how combining LLMs with software tooling can lead to faster insights.

El taller será **híbrido** y se realizará el **martes 4 de noviembre de 10:30 hs a 12:30 hs** en el edificio de INIBIOMA, sala seminarios (Pasaje Gutierrez 1400, Primer piso, Bariloche).

title: Workshop - Introduction to R
description:
After a summer break, we are kickstarting our events again with an introductory workshop on the **R** programming language. No previous knowledge of **R** is required, and we welcome all genders and identities to attend our events at R-Ladies Vienna.

**ℹ️ Abstract**
**R** is a powerful programming language and environment widely used across fields from statistics and economics to psychology, biology, and more. It also provides a versatile analytical toolkit for business analysts and professionals working with data.

The goal of this workshop is for our attendees to become acquainted with R to start their data analytics journey. Within this 1.5-hour workshop, we will cover:

* R as a calculator
* Basic data types and structures
* Reading data into R and exporting for external purposes
* Basic data processing
* Basic data visualization
* Loops, functions, and conditional statements

**💡 What to prepare**

* Before the workshop, install **R** and **RStudio** on your laptop by following the 2-step guide at [https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)
* On the workshop day, **bring your own laptop** to follow the workshop material closely and code along with us!

**⏱️ Duration**
The workshop will last approximately 1.5 hours. Doors open at 17:45.

**🗺️ Location**
Seminar Room DA green 03A / Sem.R. DA grün 03 A
(DA03A15)
3rd floor, green area
TU Wien Freihaus ([map](https://maps.app.goo.gl/N8PSCZfdi97KirFY6))
Wiedner Hauptstraße 8-10, 1040 Vienna

**📍 How to find us**
In the green area of TU Wien Freihaus, take the lift to the 3rd floor. After exiting the lift, you will see the seminar room on your right.
Use this [floor plan](https://maps.tuwien.ac.at/?q=DA04E10) to help locate the room.

title: Efficient File Management in R with {fs}
description: Join us for a hands-on workshop using the **{fs}** package to easily bring order to messy file systems. Through practical examples, you’ll learn to automate common tasks like creating, renaming, moving, and deleting files and directories. Together, we’ll transform a cluttered folder filled with mixed-case filenames and inconsistent delimiters into a clean, organized structure. By the end, you’ll have practical, reusable tools to streamline file management in your own projects.

title: Open Lecture - Topic: Basics of Graph Theory and NetworkX in Python
description:
*Open Lecture - Special Session of the “From 0 to Hero” Series*
Topic: **Basics of Graph Theory and NetworkX in Python**

👩‍🏫 Speaker: **Silva Bashllari**
Data Scientist • Researcher • Tech Community Builder

Data Science goes far beyond Machine Learning, and Graph Theory is a powerful example of that. In this open lecture, we will explore how graphs can be used to model complex systems such as social networks, transportation routes, communication infrastructures, and even the spread of misinformation online.

We will connect Graph Theory with Linear Algebra and demonstrate how key concepts are interpreted through mathematical structures. All theoretical concepts will be brought to life through hands-on modeling in Python using the NetworkX library for network analysis and graph visualization.

📍 Location: University Metropolitan Tirana, Building 3, Auditorium A2
📅 Date: 29.10.2025
🕦 Time: 11:30 – 13:00

We look forward to your participation, don’t miss it!

title: R package development with GitHub Pages and pkgdown
description:
Creating clear, professional documentation is key to making your R package useful to other people, but building a documentation website can be tricky. The [`pkgdown`](https://pkgdown.r-lib.org/) package makes this process much easier, but deploying via GitHub Pages can present its own set of challenges.

In this workshop, we’ll build a simple R package together, and use GitHub Actions and GitHub Pages to create a `pkgdown` documentation website for the package. By the end of this workshop, you’ll have:

* 🌎 A toy R package available on GitHub for others to install
* 📄 A `pkgdown` website for your package, deployed via GitHub Pages

**Before the workshop participants should:**

* Create a [GitHub account](https://github.com). Note that prior experience with package development and/or GitHub is not required.
* Download and install R, Positron, and Git on their personal PCs.
* If you have trouble with the above we are able to provide access to the Posit Cloud environment as a backup.

**At the workshop participants should:**

* Bring your own PC.
* Be willing to learn and contribute to a safe and welcoming environment.

**This is an in-person event with limited space! Please only RSVP if you are able to attend in-person!**

**\*\*\*Please note that the mission of R-Ladies is to increase gender diversity in the R community. This event is intended to provide a safe space for women and gender minorities. We ask for male allies to be invited by and accompanied by a woman or gender minority.\*\*\***

title: Lunch & Learn with Alex Velez: From data to story
description: The typical graph in a business communication often leaves our audience confused and overwhelmed. In this session, you’ll be walked through an illustration of how to avoid analysis paralysis by turning a regular business graph into an effective visual narrative. Attendees will leave with an enhanced appreciation for the power of data storytelling and practical tips they can implement immediately to achieve greater impact when communicating in a data-driven way.

title: desencapsulado()

description:
October is Breast Cancer Awareness Month — a time to raise awareness, honour the millions of lives impacted by breast cancer, and reaffirm our global commitment to equitable access to care and improved survival for all.
This webinar, “Coding Against Cancer: Using R to Detect Breast Cancer from Anthropometric Data,” brings together the worlds of data science and public health to demonstrate how coding can contribute to early detection and better health outcomes.

title: Cómo encontrar y corregir errores en tus funciones en R (Debugging Tools)
description:
Si escribís funciones pero no estás seguro de cuáles son las estrategias más eficientes para **identificar el origen de los errores y corregirlos**, ¡sumate a este taller y desbloqueá tu superpoder de programación con **técnicas de depuración**!

En este meetup, revisaremos consejos para solucionar problemas en el código, hablaremos sobre funciones de depuración (traceback(), browser(), debug(), trace() y recover()), y distinguiremos entre estrategias para depurar tu propio código y el de otra persona.

**Objetivos de aprendizaje:**

* Revisar consejos para la solución de problemas en el código.
* Aplicar funciones de depuración (traceback(), browser(), debug(), trace() y recover()) e identificar beneficios adicionales de usar algunas de estas estrategias dentro de RStudio.
* Distinguir entre estrategias para depurar tu propio código y el de otra persona.

**¡Importante!:**
El taller es en inglés, pero estaremos las organizadoras del capítulo de Buenos Aires para traducir preguntas y ayudar en el chat.
Traé tu RStudio y R actualizados para poder seguir el taller desde tu computadora y sacarle mayor provecho.

title: BNPL and Machine Learning: Decisions That Scale
description:
In this presentation, we’ll explore how machine learning models are transforming decision-making in the BNPL (Buy Now, Pay Later) landscape. Focusing on credit scoring, we’ll discuss the unique challenges of this audience—such as lack of credit history, short-term behavior, and high volatility—and how predictive techniques can deliver fast, scalable, and accurate responses.
The talk will cover everything from feature engineering to performance evaluation in dynamic environments, while also addressing ethical considerations and financial inclusion. By the end, participants will gain a clear understanding of how data and algorithms can drive smarter, safer decisions in instant credit.

* el origen de este movimiento que busca visibilizar y reconocer el rol de quienes escriben código para proyectos de investigación
* cómo usa R alguien que trabaja como RSE
* los principales temas y aprendizajes que nos dejó la RSECon, la conferencia que organiza la Society of RSE.

¡Te esperamos!

title: Comunicação efetiva com dados: da exploração à explanação
description:
A proposta é conduzir os participantes por **quatro perspectivas complementares**:

1. **Dados** – entender a qualidade, estrutura e contexto do conjunto de dados antes de qualquer visualização.
2. **Exploração** – experimentar opções gráficas e análises para descobrir padrões e relações.
3. **Explanação** – escolher formas de apresentação que tornem a informação clara, atraente e adequada ao público.
4. **Narrativa** – conectar tudo em uma história que engaje, sem perder a precisão.

O objetivo é ir além do chamado *data storytelling* tradicional, mostrando que comunicar bem exige atenção desde a preparação dos dados até a construção da narrativa final. É um curso que une fundamentos de visualização (como o uso do **ggplot** no R) a decisões de design e de storytelling, ajudando quem trabalha com dados a transformar análises em mensagens realmente impactantes.

Nos siga nas redes sociais: @rladiesgyn
#VemAí #MistérioNoConsole

title: NASA Space Apps 2025 in Zurich
description:
NASA Space Apps Challenge 2025 – Zurich second year edition.

For the second time, after succesful event in 2024, and more than 10 projects submitted locally, Switzerland in Zurich again will host the NASA Space Apps Challenge, where innovators, developers, scientists, designers, students, and creatives come together to solve real challenges on Earth and in space using open data from NASA and partner space agencies.
Date: October 4–5, 2025
Location: Zurich (offline & online participation possible) Global Theme: *Solving for Earth with Space*

### What is Space Apps?

NASA Space Apps is a 48-hour global hackathon that engages tens of thousands of participants across 150+ countries. Teams work on challenges covering areas such as:

* Earth observation & climate action
* Space exploration & robotics
* AI, data & technology for science
* Human health & future of life in space

Whether you’re a coder, designer, engineer, storyteller, or space enthusiast, you’ll find a challenge where your skills make a difference.

### Why Join in Zurich?

* Be part of the first-ever Zurich edition
* Connect with a global community of innovators
* Collaborate in interdisciplinary teams
* Access NASA’s open data and resources
* Win local prizes & compete for global recognition by NASA

### Who Can Join?

Everyone is welcome — from high school students to professionals, beginners to experts. No previous space or coding experience required — just curiosity, creativity, and teamwork!

**How to Register**

1. Click Register and create account at the platform www.spaceappschallenge.org
2. Chose Zurich location and fill small form there https://linkly.link/2E8Px Closer to the date, you’ll receive access to the official NASA Space Apps platform to select challenges and form teams.
3. After that you are all set for the hackathon!

Let’s solve for Earth and space — together in Zurich!

Learn more about the global program:
www.spaceappschallenge.org

Venue update. The hackathon will be in Community event space, final awards at the big Conference hall. Registration for the hackathon at the official website, please create account and choose Zurich as your location :https://linkly.link/2E8Px. Registration is mandatory

title: Nuevo Meetup R-Ladies Bariloche: Let's talk!
description:
¡¡¡ATENCION!!!!
Tenemos el agrado de invitarlas a la presentación y discusión de una publicación reciente del grupo de R-Ladies Bariloche en el número especial de Ecología Austral: [La dimensión de género en la ecología: visiones desde la diversidad de enfoques](https://ojs.ecologiaaustral.com.ar/index.php/Ecologia_Austral/announcement/view/11)
\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-\-
El artículo, producto de nuestra historia compartida con ustedes y otras R-Ladies del mundo, se titula ***Valores compartidos en la comunidad R-Ladies: Hacia la construcción de un liderazgo alternativo al propuesto por el modelo patriarcal***

Queremos invitarlas a discutir el artículo en comunidad para seguir pensando otras formas de liderar, desde nuestros valores, experiencias y objetivos de vida.

Resumen del artículo:
"Aún existe una brecha de género importante en muchas áreas del conocimiento, incluyendo la ecología. Si bien hoy existen más oportunidades de liderazgo que antes para los géneros subrepresentados, éstas —frecuentemente vinculadas a contextos masculinizados, verticalistas y centrados en la productividad y el poder— fuerzan a las personas a desistir de ocupar dichos roles o bien a reproducir la misma dinámica asumiendo grandes costos personales. Así, existe la necesidad urgente de crear oportunidades de liderazgo —distintas a aquellas que nos ofrece el modelo capitalista patriarcal— para que los géneros subrepresentados puedan desarrollar sus capacidades, compartir saberes y ser motores de cambio hacia un ambiente académico-científico creativo, reflexivo e inclusivo. Para esto es clave poner en valor las experiencias que surgen de espacios cuyo objetivo es crear ámbitos de crecimiento respetuosos y justos. R-Ladies, es una red global colaborativa que busca promover la diversidad de géneros en el ámbito de la programación en el software libre R, alentando y apoyando a los géneros subrepresentados (mujeres cis/trans, varones trans, no binaries, etc.) para que alcancen su máximo potencial en programación. Realizamos una encuesta dentro comunidad de la comunidad de R-Ladies para recuperar valores, experiencias y estrategias para construir nuevos modelos de liderazgo. Como valores fundamentales para un modelo de liderazgo alternativo identificamos la capacidad de: 1) escuchar, empatizar y valorar a cada persona integrante del equipo independientemente de su posición o trayectoria; 2) acompañar e incentivar a otres a alcanzar sus objetivos, y 3) liderar desde el respeto, la inclusión y la diversidad. Creemos imprescindible la formación en perspectiva de género de las personas que ocupan posiciones de liderazgo y la no precarización del trabajo de les otres. Las redes que fomentan la participación de géneros subrepresentados, como R-Ladies, ofrecen una comunidad y promueven un espacio seguro y respetuoso para el aprendizaje."

El encuentro será presencial y se realizará el **jueves 2 de octubre de 11 hs a 12 hs en el edificio de INIBIOMA, sala de seminarios (Pasaje Gutiérrez 1400, Bariloche).**

Está vez no hace falta tener conocimiento de R, pero sí el artículo leído ([acceder al artículo](https://ojs.ecologiaaustral.com.ar/index.php/Ecologia_Austral/article/view/2474)) y acordar con el [Código de conducta](https://github.com/rladies/.github/blob/master/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md#spanish) que es necesario aceptar a partir del link.

A quienes asistan, recuerden traer su taza/mate y si quieren, algo para compartir!!

¡Importante! title: Using Python Within Stata
description:
🐍✨ **This time it’s Python time… inside Stata!**
Discover how to unlock new possibilities by combining two powerful tools in one workflow.

**Python** is an increasingly popular programming language that can be used for many statistical and nonstatistical applications. **Stata** provides a bidirectional connection with Python using the **Stata Function Interface (SFI)**.

This talk will demonstrate **how to use Python interactively within Stata**, how to execute Python scripts within Stata, how to use Python in Stata do-files, and how to integrate Python into Stata commands and .ado files.

No prior experience with Python is necessary.

title: R package creation made easy: Functions!
description:
Do you want to learn how to develop R packages from scratch? This R-Ladies Cologne series is just for you! Together, we’ll walk through **every step of package development**, from writing your first function to submitting your package to CRAN.

To launch this series, our guest Sébastien Rochette introduced his package **{fusen}**, which turns R Markdown files into fully functional R packages. You can watch the first workshop here: https://youtu.be/wAkZvwPK1P4?si=w_dYxTwhLKRxGtzz

In this second meeting, the R-Ladies Cologne team is organizing a hands-on workshop on functions - the building blocks of packages!

We will teach you:

* How do functions work?
* What are dependencies, and how to address them?
* How to document your functions with Roxygen?

You will then collaborate in teams to build functions for a real package that will be submitted to CRAN in the final meeting of this series. It does not matter if you are a beginner or experienced R user, this workshop series welcomes all!

**When?**
📅 Date: 29.08.2025
🕒 Time: 3:00pm CEST
📍 Location: Online (access details will be shared with RSVPs)

We can’t wait to see many of you there! Let’s build packages together. 💪👩‍💻

\*\*\*

To stay up-to-date with our future events, make sure to also follow us on Bluesky (@rladies-cologne.bsky.social) and Fosstodon (@rladies_cologne@fosstodon.org)

*This workshop is part of the R-Ladies Cologne series on "Toolbox for Reproducibility", where we dive into tips, tricks, and techniques on how to adhere to best standards and make our code and workflows reproducible.*

title: Topic Modeling: Turning Conversation into Strategy
description:
Imagine you are a Data Scientist working for a global e-commerce giant. Every day, millions of customers leave feedback—ranging from product reviews to support chat transcripts—covering everything from delivery delays to product quality concerns. These comments, written in natural language, pile up into hundreds of thousands of unstructured records each month. Your task is to sift through this vast sea of text, uncover the most common themes, spot emerging issues, and share actionable recommendations with marketing, operations, and product teams—decisions that could directly shape customer satisfaction and business growth.
This type of real-world challenge highlights the power of cutting-edge techniques like topic modeling. This tool transforms raw conversation into strategic insight, turning scattered voices into a clear, data-driven direction.
Topic Modeling: Turning Conversation into Strategy explores how unstructured text—such as customer feedback, social media discussions, call center transcripts, or survey responses—can be analyzed to reveal recurring themes and priorities. By applying algorithms like Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) organizations can detect trends, identify pain points, and prioritize actions.
This process bridges the gap between what people are saying and what an organization needs to do, ensuring that decisions are informed by evidence rather than guesswork.

description:
En este nuevo taller, **[Betsy Cohen](https://www.linkedin.com/in/betsabe1984/?locale=en_US)** nos mostrará cómo crear **[tableros o dashboards ](https://quarto.org/docs/dashboards/)con Quarto**, una herramienta de publicación científica y técnica que integra código, texto y visualizaciones de forma reproducible.

Betsy nos presentará las principales funcionalidades de esta herramientas para la generación de dashboards estáticos en HTML que podemos publicar fácil y rápido.

**¡Bonus extra! Vamos a sumarle un poquito de magia interactiva con el paquete [crosstalk](https://rstudio.github.io/crosstalk/) y su [kpi widget](https://arnold-kakas.github.io/kpiwidget/)**

\> ⚠️ Si no conocés Quarto\, no te preocupes: te recomendamos [este taller introductorio](https://youtu.be/puykTjJ43Xs?si=PFXkdKzEnA58PQ1C) para ponerte al día antes del encuentro.

**Requerimientos:**

title: Cerveja com Dados Porto Alegre
description:
**INSCREVA-SE** [ ](https://ok.org.br/projetos/cerveja-com-dados/)[http://bit.ly/3GrACBL](http://bit.ly/3GrACBL)

*Dessa vez, nosso encontro será diferente!*

O **Cerveja com Dados Porto Alegre** voltou em uma edição especial sobre **crise climática e catástrofes ambientais**, com foco no Rio Grande do Sul. E, como sempre, com entrada gratuita!

O evento, realizado em parceria com **R-Ladies Porto Alegre**, **Verifica RS** e **AfonteJor**, é uma oportunidade imperdível para debater **como o uso de dados** pode ser nossa ferramenta mais poderosa para entender e enfrentar os desafios do clima.

* **“Climatologia subtropical: como o hemisfério sul influencia no clima do Rio Grande do Sul”, por Pedro Valente**
Pedro é geógrafo e climatologista pela UFRGS. Atualmente, realiza pós-doutorado no Centro Polar e Climático (UFRGS) e na Universidade de Ohio (Estados Unidos). Estuda as conexões trópico-polo (climatologia polar e subtropical) e seus impactos no hemisfério sul durante os séculos XIX, XX e XXI.
* **“Explorando o papel funcional da biodiversidade na resiliência climática”, por Felícia Miranda Fischer**
Felícia é bióloga, mestre e doutora em Ecologia pela UFRGS, e já trabalhou como pesquisadora na Masaryk University na República Tcheca e no Centro de Investigaciones sobre Desertificación na Espanha. Hoje, é pesquisadora do projeto Programa de Pesquisa em Biodiversidade (PPBIO) no departamento de Ecologia da UFRGS. Atua na área de ecologia funcional, tentando entender a dinâmica dos organismos vivos, principalmente as plantas, e como respondem a distúrbios antrópicos e climáticos.
* **“Cidades Mais Resilientes Em Contextos Metropolitanos”, por Geisa Rorato**
Geisa é arquiteta e urbanista (UFSM), doutora em Planejamento Urbano e Regional (PROPUR/UFRGS) e professora do Departamento de Urbanismo da Faculdade de Arquitetura da UFRGS. Integra o Programa de Pós-Graduação em Planejamento Urbano e Regional (PROPUR) e o Programa de Pós-Graduação em Políticas Públicas (PPGPP), ambos da UFRGS, e o Observatório das Metrópoles – Núcleo Porto Alegre. Tem experiência em planejamento urbano e regional, e na elaboração de planos diretores e de planos setoriais. Tem realizado pesquisas sobre as relações interfederativas e o planejamento urbano e regional, e sobre as mudanças climáticas e o planejamento urbano e regional.

Junte-se a especialistas para palestras e discussões aprofundadas!

**Evento:** Cerveja com Dados Porto Alegre 2025
**Tema:** Crise Climática
**Data:** 13 de agosto de 2025 (quarta-feira)
**Horário:** 19h30
**Local:** Justo - Escadarias da Borges de Medeiros 741, Passeio Verão, Porto Alegre, Rio Grande do Sul 90010-241

PROMO DO JUSTO: o Justo vai dar 10% de desconto em todo o menu de bebidas durante a noite toda para quem estiver no evento!

title: Comunicá tus datos con R y Quarto
description:
En este primer taller [Jesica Formoso](https://www.linkedin.com/in/jesica-formoso-16ab4649?miniProfileUrn=urn%3Ali%3Afs_miniProfile%3AACoAAApQQWIBZ1n05jDUJcj-DQMhDILgsmCioec&lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Ad_flagship3_search_srp_all%3BryFvB3gTSziFYqrVglcEbg%3D%3D) nos presentará las principales funcionalidades de [Quarto](https://quarto.org/), una herramienta de publicación de documentos científicos y técnicos que te permitirán comunicar tus hallazgos en informes, presentaciones y sitios web.

Junto a Jesica, exploraremos ejemplos prácticos de su uso en informes reproducibles, abordando temas como la integración de código y texto, la personalización de formatos de salida y el uso de parámetros para crear variantes de documentos.

Este taller es **introductorio** está dirigida a personas con un **conocimiento básico de R y poca o ninguna experiencia previa con Quarto**.

**Requerimientos:**

* R: 4.5.1
* Quarto: v1.7 (descargar quarto CLI [aquí](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/))
* RStudio: 2025.05.1 (descargar [aquí](https://posit.co/downloads/))
* Instalar el paquete **tidyverse**

IMPORTANTE:
Este es un evento presencial por lo que los cupos son limitados.

title: Introducción a R: desde cero a tu primer análisis de datos (EDA)
description:
Hiromi Kameyama diseñó este taller para quienes desean iniciarse en el uso de **R** y dar sus primeros pasos en el análisis de datos. A lo largo de la sesión aprenderás a manejar R de forma sencilla, desde su instalación y uso básico, hasta realizar un **análisis exploratorio de datos (EDA)** completo.

Trabajaremos paso a paso para:
✅ Entender la interfaz de R y RStudio.
✅ Importar y manipular datos de forma clara.
✅ Aplicar transformaciones básicas y limpieza de datos.
✅ Generar visualizaciones descriptivas.
✅ Extraer conclusiones iniciales para tus propios proyectos.

No necesitas experiencia previa: **empezaremos desde cero**, con explicaciones accesibles y ejemplos prácticos. Al final del taller tendrás las herramientas necesarias para comenzar a explorar y analizar tus propios conjuntos de datos usando R.

title: From Maps to Insights: Geospatial Hotspot Analysis in R
description:
**Are you a public health professional, data analyst, student, or researcher eager to unlock the power of location-based insights?**

Join us for this hands-on training designed to equip you with practical skills in geospatial data analysis using R.
Using real-world data, you’ll learn how to import and visualize shapefiles, merge health data, perform spatial clustering analysis, and detect disease hotspots using Local Indicators of Spatial Association (LISA).
This training will guide you ***step-by-step*** ***from raw data to meaningful maps and spatial statistics that can inform health interventions and decision-making***.
📍 Learn. Map. Analyze. Interpret.
**No prior GIS experience required, just a passion for learning!**

title: Using Git for Version Control and Collaboration
description:
Do you have trouble keeping track of changes in your code? Have you ever had to collaborate on a project with others and had to send files back and forth tediously? **Git** will be your best friend to solve these problems and many more!

This presentation will give you an introduction to **Git**, the basics of how to use it, and some of its very helpful applications such as collaboration and code reproducibility. The focus will be on the use of Git in conjunction with Github (yes, these are two separate things!). This will help you to effectively use one of the most powerful tools available to you and give you more confidence to code freely without fear.

**There’ll also be food, drinks, and plenty of time to network with fellow attendees!**

title: Forecasting Time Series with Linear Regression: A Feature-Driven Approach
description:
Curious about time series forecasting but not sure where to start?

Join us for a beginner-friendly workshop exploring how linear regression can be used for time series forecasting.

This talk explores ***the fundamentals of time series forecasting using linear regression models***. We’ll cover how to transform time series data into a supervised learning format and utilize feature engineering techniques to model various components of the series, including trends, seasons, outliers, and structural breaks.

No prior time series experience is required—just a basic understanding of regression and a desire to learn.

🧠 **What you’ll learn:**

* How to frame time series as a regression problem
* Feature engineering techniques for time-aware data
* Modeling trend, seasonality, outliers, and breaks
* Practical tips for evaluation and validation

Whether you’re brushing up on regression or starting your forecasting journey, this workshop will provide hands-on insights in an accessible way.

title: From Code to Crisis Response: Using R for Real-World Impact [ONLINE]
description:
Please join us for an online meetup with [Kelly Charniga](https://bsky.app/profile/kcharniga.bsky.social) on applying R to public health challenges while serving as a Prevention Effectiveness Fellow at the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC).

**Topic:** As a Prevention Effectiveness Fellow at the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC), Kelly had the opportunity to apply R to interesting public health challenges. In this talk, she'll share two projects that show how R moves beyond academic exercises into work that directly impacts public health.
First, she'll walk through how she used existing R packages to model mpox transmission potential ahead of the 2022 global outbreak. You'll see the practical side of epidemiological modelling—the packages she relied on, the data challenges she faced, and how the results foreshadowed the current mpox epidemic in Africa.
Next, she'll discuss developing an RShiny app for rabies risk assessments that's now being used across the U.S. She'll share the development process, from initial concept to deployment, including the lessons learned about building tools for non-technical end users.
Whether you're curious about public health applications of R, interested in Shiny development, or wondering how your R skills might translate to government work, this talk will give you concrete examples and practical insights you can apply to your own projects.

**Disclaimer:** Kelly is not a current CDC employee and speaks in a personal capacity. Her views are her own and do not represent the CDC or the US Government.

**Prerequisites:** There are no prerequisites. All members are encouraged to attend.

**Code of Conduct:** R-Ladies is dedicated to providing a harassment-free experience for everyone. We do not tolerate harassment of participants in any form. For more information, see the [code of conduct](https://rladies.org/coc/).

title: Lunch&Learn with Ella Kaye: C for R users
description:
Much of base R is written in C. As R users, we may encounter this code when debugging our own code. As R contributors, we may wish to propose patches to the C code if we discover a bug. In any case, learning a new language can be fun and rewarding!
In this talk, I'll discuss why, as R users/programmers, we may want to learn C, and resources for doing so. I'll show examples of how C is used in the codebase of base R. I'll give an example of how, with only a little C knowledge, it was possible to add a new feature into the language (specifying colours with three-digit hex codes). Finally, I’ll discuss various initiatives from the R Contribution Working Group and show how you, too, can get involved in contributing to base R.

title: Programmation R efficace en temps et en mémoire
description:
R-Ladies Paris en collaboration avec R Addicts vous donne rendez-vous le vendredi 27 juin à 12H30 CEST pour un événement en direct sur Zoom ! Nous aurons le plaisir d’accueillir Toby Dylan Hocking, Associate professor of Computer Science, qui partagera son expertise sur la programmation R efficace en temps et en mémoire.

**Résumé**
Vous devez souvent attendre que le code R calcule des résultats à partir de grands ensembles de données ? Ou êtes-vous à court de mémoire ? Toby Dylan Hocking nous montrera la meilleure manière de remanier le code R afin de minimiser l'utilisation du temps et de la mémoire.
Les sujets abordés incluent le profilage du code pour déterminer quelles lignes/fonctions bénéficieraient d'une optimisation, et l'analyse comparative pour comparer l'utilisation du temps et de la mémoire de différentes versions du code (en utilisant le package ***atime*** qu'il propose).
Exemple : [Quand NE PAS écrire de code de bas niveau ?](https://github.com/tdhock/when-c?tab=readme-ov-file#when-to-not-write-low-level-code)

title: Desde hojas de calculo a R - Encuentro 2
description:
Este curso está dirigido a personas que utilizan hojas de cálculo para la manipulación de datos y que nunca han programado, pero que les gustaría aprender a trabajar con R. Por lo tanto, comenzamos por el principio: ¿por qué usar R? y ¿por dónde empezar?
Proponemos trabajar con R de forma ordenada y reproducible. Por ello, presentamos un flujo de trabajo que permite a quienes realicen este curso aplicar buenas prácticas de programación, trabajar de forma colaborativa y presentar su trabajo en un único documento que incluya el análisis y los resultados.
Siempre que podamos, mencionaremos cómo lo que proponemos resolver con R también se puede hacer con hojas de cálculo y las ventajas y desventajas de cada enfoque.
En cada sección incluimos actividades junto con ejemplos. Queremos que estos ejercicios sean realistas para que cualquiera pueda encontrar similitudes en sus propios datos y pueda aplicar lo aprendido a otras situaciones.

title: Speaking Many Languages: Finding Power and Joy in R and Python
description:
**Join us for a special [PyData](https://www.meetup.com/it-IT/pydata-roma-capitale/) x [R-Ladies](rladiesrome.org) x [Rome R Users Group](https://www.meetup.com/rome-r-users-group/events/308302187)** **EVENT!**

**IN PERSON at Istituto Nazionale di Geofisica e Vulcanologia -** **[INGV.IT](ingv.it)**
**Registration for in person**: **[https://www.meetup.com/pydata-roma-capitale/events/308302003](https://www.meetup.com/pydata-roma-capitale/events/308302003)**
⚠️ Remember to RSVP using your **full name** for security reasons and bring a **valid ID** to show at the entrance. **Otherwise, you will not be allowed to enter the premises!** ⚠️

**ONLINE Attendees can use the zoom link on this page.**

We’ll kick off the day with a warm welcome from [R-Ladies](rladiesrome.org) & [Rome R Users](https://www.meetup.com/rome-r-users-group/) communities, followed by an inspiring opening talk and a series of dynamic 5-minute **lightning talks** from community members. Whether you’re joining in person or tuning in remotely, we’re excited to celebrate the power of open-source languages, data science, and community.

Schedule:

* 18:00 🚪 Doors open
* 18:15 🙌 Welcome from PyData Roma Capitale, R-Ladies Rome, and R User Group
* 18.30 🎤 **"Speaking Many Languages: Finding Power and Joy in R and Python" – Federica Gazzelloni**
* 19.00 ⚡ **Lightning Talks** (open mic, up to 5 minutes!)
* 19.30 🤝 Networking —share insights, spark new collaborations
* 20:00 👋 Close & goodnight (or if you want, join us for a multi-community dinner outside the venue!)

**Opening Talk – Speaking Many Languages: Finding Power and Joy in R and Python**
*By Federica Gazzelloni*
When I first started my journey in data science, I believed I had to “choose a side”—R or Python. But over time, I discovered that speaking more than one programming language isn’t about choosing sides; it’s about expanding perspectives. In this talk, I’ll share a bit of my personal story—how I came to learn both R and Python, what each has taught me, and how switching between them has enriched my work and opened unexpected doors. Along the way, I’ll walk through a few simple, side-by-side examples in both languages to highlight their strengths and unique styles. Whether you’re deeply rooted in one language or just starting out, I hope this talk leaves you feeling empowered to learn broadly, build confidently, and connect across communities.

title: RLadies x PyLadies Vancouver Career Panel
description:
RLadies and PyLadies Vancouver are teaming up to bring you a panel of professionals to discuss career paths and current challenges. Whether you are starting out in your career or a seasoned veteran, the information covered will help you in your career! Bring your questions and notepad!

Here is a sneak peak at some of the questions we will be asking:
-What skills should a new data scientist have if trying to get a job as a data scientist/data engineer/data analyst
-What are some of the most important skills for a data scientist to have on the job?

We will also have dedicated time for the audience to ask questions as well.

**Panelist Profiles:**
*Momoko Ueda*
Momoko Ueda, PhD is the Founding Data Scientist at Taskmole, where she uses Python, machine learning and large language models to discover and automate repetitive workflows. She holds a PhD in Criminology with a focus on forensic science, and previously worked at a startup focused on threat and misinformation detection on social media. She’s also the Co-founder of HirePhD Career Society, a nonprofit that built a global community of 6,000+ PhDs to support their transition into industry.

*Shannon Lo*
Shannon is a data professional who has worked in various industries including telecom, retail, and public health. Her current work involves topic-specific analyses on the Canadian Coroner and Medical Examiner Database. When she's not geeking out about data, you can usually find her hiking, climbing, or camping.

*Kathryn Morrison*
Kathryn Morrison is a statistician and the founder of Precision Analytics, a boutique firm specializing in health data science. She has over a decade of experience applying statistical methods in industry, such as health technology assessment and real-world evidence projects. Kathryn works primarily in R, developing custom analytics pipelines, statistical models, and reproducible workflows for clients ranging from pharmaceutical companies to research institutes. She also serves on committees for the Statistical Society of Canada and co-organizes the R-Ladies Montreal chapter.

More to come!

***

1. If this is your first R-Ladies event, please take a moment to review our R-Ladies Global code of conduct: https://github.com/rladies/starter-kit/wiki/Code-of-Conduct
2. Who is this allowed to attend the event?
Everyone is welcome to join us at our events and to be a part of our mission to empower gender minorities with the skills and knowledge to code in one of the top programming language in data science, which is R.
3. To fulfill this mission we do ask participants to respect a focus on the encouragement and support for women and non-binary people during our events.

See you there!

Jasmine, Melissa and Mariatta

description:
Join fellow R-Ladies in exploring a #tidytuesday data set! For those craving human connection and some practice flexing your data science skills, this is the event for you! We will hold our event via Zoom, where you can meet other R-ladies, improve your R skills, and build your data science portfolio. This event is for people at all levels of R (beginner through advanced).

At this event, we'll revisit one of the Tidy Tuesday datasets from January 2025 on the US Census Bureau water insecurity data (yes, let's try to do some spatial mapping!). You can read more about it here: https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2025/2025-01-28/readme.md and see some previous analysis of the data by folks at the US Geological Survey here: https://waterdata.usgs.gov/blog/acs-maps/.

6:15 - 7:15 pm #tidytuesday

What is #tidytuesday?
#TidyTuesday is a weekly social data project created by the R4DS online learning community that is focused on using tidyverse packages. A new data set and accompanying article featuring an interesting plot is released on Github every Monday. The goal is to generate a copy of the original plot or create your own original approach!

For more information on #tidytuesday, check out [this blog post](https://bit.ly/2HqEsdt) and [the main repository page](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday).

Who is this for?
New and experienced R users alike! Let's learn together! All are welcome, including R-Ladies RTP members and R-Ladies from around the world.

title: Scrollytelling Science with Quarto and Closeread [ONLINE]
description:
Please join us for an online meetup with [Cecilia Baldoni](https://bsky.app/profile/cecibaldoni.bsky.social) on Scrollytelling science with [Quarto](https://quarto.org/) and [Closeread](https://closeread.dev/).

**Topic:** If you enjoy building things with R and Markdown, scrollytelling might be your next favorite tool!
In this talk, Cecilia will show how she used Quarto and its Closeread format to build an interactive scrollytelling website that presents her research on brain plasticity in shrews. She will walk through the structure of a Closeread project, show how R-generated plots can be integrated, and share layout tips, and common pitfalls. This session is a hands-on look at turning any topic into a compelling visual story using tools many R users already know.

**Prerequisites:** There are no prerequisites. All members are encouraged to attend.

**Code of Conduct:** R-Ladies is dedicated to providing a harassment-free experience for everyone. We do not tolerate harassment of participants in any form. For more information, see the [code of conduct](https://rladies.org/coc/).

description:

title: Explorando o pacote {pcir} para análise de conflitos e consenso
description:
Você já precisou medir o nível de consenso ou conflito entre diferentes grupos? Que tal aprender uma maneira prática, eficiente e reprodutível de calcular e visualizar esses dados no R?

**Sobre a oficina**👩🏽‍💻
Nesta oficina, será apresentado o **{pcir}**, um pacote R desenvolvido para facilitar o cálculo, a comparação e a visualização do **Índice de Potencial de Conflito (PCI)**. Esse índice é uma ferramenta estatística essencial para entender padrões de concordância e divergência em diversas áreas, como sociologia, psicologia, ciência política, economia e gestão de recursos naturais. Além de aprender a usar o {pcir}, Francesca também compartilhará sua experiência no *rOpenSci Champions Program*, uma iniciativa que impulsiona o desenvolvimento de software aberto para pesquisa, onde teve a oportunidade de desenvolver este pacote.

title: R in Industry: Biopharmaceutical Process Optimization through Advanced DoE
description:
In our May 2025 meetup, we explore the interdisciplinary application of data science in the biopharmaceutical industry. Christina Yassouridis, Senior Data Scientist from Boehringer Ingelheim, will share with us how she designs and develops an R package which applies Design of Experiment (DoE) approaches to optimize biopharmaceutical manufacturing process.

Expect to hear about:
🟡 **Data science:** Theoretical background of advanced Design of Experiments (DoE) approach using Halton designs and Latin Hypercube Designs
🟣 **R programming:** The design and development of R package to meet industry needs
🟢 **Biopharmaceutical industry:** Current industry applications of R package in solving optimization problems

It will be an interesting sharing to see how R is applied to solve real-world problems in biopharmaceutical manufacturing, an area closely connected to our well-being. The theoretical background of the DoE approaches involved will also be introduced, which allows R users without field knowledge to follow along.

R-Ladies Vienna promotes gender diversity in the R community. **All genders and skill levels in R are welcome to our events.** We look forward to seeing you!

**🗣️ Speaker**
Christina is currently a Senior Data Scientist at Boehringer Ingelheim, one of the world’s largest pharmaceutical companies. With a PhD in Applied Statistics, Christina has extensive experience as a data science professional in both industry and consulting, and as a researcher at BOKU.

**ℹ️ Abstract**
Biopharmaceutical manufacturing encompasses complex processes involving living organisms and multiple interacting steps. To optimize these processes, data scientists develop digital representations using machine learning models to identify optimal process parameters across multidimensional experimental spaces. For Gaussian process regression models, evenly distributed experimental points yield superior results.

The R package {spacefillngDoEs}, developed by Christina, integrates and extends two established approaches:

1. **Halton designs:** These low-discrepancy sequences enable efficient quasi-Monte Carlo methods for numerical integration and sampling in high-dimensional spaces.
2. **Latin Hypercube Designs**: This statistical method generates near-random parameter value samples from multidimensional distributions.

By combining functionalities from the R packages {qrng} and {SLHD} while extending their capabilities to handle mixed data types, {spacefillngDoEs} addresses specific industry requirements.

In this presentation, Christina will introduce the theoretical foundations of generalized Halton designs, demonstrate the R package's functionality, and showcase its current industrial application.

**⏱️ Duration**
The event will last approximately 1 to 1.5 hours, including a Q&A session. Doors open at 17:45.

**🗺️ Location**
Seminar Room DA green 04 / Seminarraum DA grün 04 (DA04E10)
4th floor, green area

title: Cross-chapter: TidyTuesday with R-Ladies
description:
Come join us for a **fun session on coding with R and Rstudio**!
R-Ladies Canberra, Melbourne and Sydney are thrilled to host our first ever cross-chapter ***TidyTuesday*** and we would love to have you come along!

TidyTuesday is a weekly event created by the Data Science Learning Community for folks to develop new skills in a low-stakes, welcoming environment. Each week, a new dataset is released for everyone to play and explore. There is no limit to what you can do! You can produce a nice-looking table, or a story-telling graphic, perhaps even an intuitive Shiny interface – the choice is yours!

**What to expect:**
Join a follow-along group or work independently on the same dataset for about 30 mins then come together to celebrate each other’s creations!
*This event is online only. All you need is your laptop!*

\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*\*

We do not tolerate harassment of event participants in any form, for any event venue, including talks, workshops, parties, Twitter and other online media.\*

title: Intro to Geospatial Tools in R
description:
Curious about working with spatial data in R? Whether you're just starting out or looking to sharpen your skills and learn about new tools\* this R Ladies meetup is for you!

\*There have been some new packages introduced to work with spatial data in R (sf, terra) in the past few years, and older packages have been phased out (rgdal, raster). This meetup will focus on the new packages, with some helpful comparisons for those who may have learned using the older tools.

**We will cover:**

* An introduction to popular R packages for spatial analysis (like sf, raster, terra, and mapview)
* Tools for static and interactive spatial data visualization in R (tmap, leaflet)
* Live-coding demonstrations of mapping, data wrangling, and spatial visualization
* How R fits into the larger GIS Tools and spatial data visualization ecosystem

**Please bring your laptop with R and R studio installed.**

**🗓️ Date:** May 5th, 2025
**🕕 Time:** 6:00 - 7:00 pm
**📍 Location:** Purple Box @ Entrepreneurs Sandbox, 643 Ilalo Street, Honolulu, HI
(we also hope to have a hybrid option, and will send out a link closer to the date)

Thank you to **Sandbox, HUB and HTDC** for generously sponsoring the space for this meeting!

title: Conferencia: Mujeres en Ciencia de Datos – Aprendizaje Práctico con R
description:
**Conferencia: Mujeres en Ciencia de Datos – Aprendizaje Práctico con R**

**Fecha y Horario:** 2 De Mayo Sábado, de 10:00 AM a 3:00 PM

**Descripción:**

Este evento está diseñado para introducir a mujeres estudiantes y principiantes en el mundo de la Ciencia de Datos, proporcionando un enfoque práctico con la programación en R. A lo largo de la conferencia, combinaremos charlas inspiradoras, talleres interactivos y sesiones de networking para fomentar el aprendizaje y la participación de mujeres en la tecnología y el análisis de datos.

**Agenda del Evento**

**10:00 – 10:30 \| Registro y Bienvenida**

Presentación del evento y objetivos generales. Breve introducción sobre la importancia de la Ciencia de Datos y la participación de mujeres en el área.

**10:30 – 11:30 \| Charla: "Mujeres en Ciencia de Datos: Oportunidades y Retos"**

* Experiencias y trayectorias en ciencia de datos.
* Aplicaciones de la Ciencia de Datos en diversas industrias.
* Recomendaciones para mujeres que inician en esta disciplina.
* **11:30 – 13:00 \| Taller: "Introducción a R para Ciencia de Datos"**
* Instalación y configuración de R y RStudio.
* Sintaxis básica del lenguaje R.
* Manipulación de datos con `dplyr` y `tidyr`.
* Visualización de datos con `ggplot2`.
* **13:00 – 13:30 \| Receso y Networking**

Oportunidad para conectar con otras participantes, compartir experiencias y explorar recursos para continuar aprendiendo.

**13:30 – 14:30 \| Panel de Discusión: "Primeros Pasos y Crecimiento en Ciencia de Datos"**

* Discusión con profesionales y estudiantes sobre estrategias de aprendizaje y desarrollo de carrera en Ciencia de Datos.
* Recursos recomendados (cursos, libros, comunidades y mentorías).
* **14:30 – 15:00 \| Cierre y Conclusiones**
* Reflexión sobre aprendizajes adquiridos.
* Presentación de próximos eventos y oportunidades de formación.
* Entrega de certificados de participación.

**Dirigido a:**

Mujeres estudiantes, profesionales y entusiastas de la Ciencia de Datos que deseen iniciar su camino en el análisis de datos con R. No se requiere experiencia previa.

title: Git and GitHub for R Users
description:

Contents

* Understand why version control is necessary and useful.
* Be able to collaborate on code using Git and GitHub from RStudio.
* Edit code, track changes, and review code using GitHub.

Target Audience

* You’re (reasonably) comfortable writing R code to do some analysis but…
* Your scripts are getting longer and longer
* There are R scripts everywhere
* You don’t want other people to see the code you’ve written because it’s all a bit of a mess!
* You don’t have any experience of Git or GitHub but you’d like to get started.

Prerequisites

* Basic knowledge of R (including know what R projects are) and RStudio.

Zoom link will be provided closer to the event.

title: Un meilleur historique Git, sans difficulté
description:
R-Ladies Paris vous donne rendez-vous le vendredi 11 avril à 12H CEST pour un événement en direct sur Zoom ! Nous aurons le plaisir d’accueillir Maëlle Salmon, Research Software Engineer, qui partagera son expertise sur Git.

**Résumé :** "Une pratique confiante de git peut changer la vie professionnelle de quiconque écrit du code ou de la prose avec R. Avec git on obtient un historique utile à parcourir ou à consulter, la possibilité de travailler en parallèle sur différents aspects, etc. En particulier, un conseil récurrent sur git est de créer de petits commits atomiques avec des messages informatifs. Pourquoi ? Et comment ?
Apprenez trois avantages des petits commits git. Découvrez comment les créer de manière réaliste, sans trop de tracas. Découvrez comment le paquet R saperlipopette peut vous aider à vous entraîner en toute sécurité, et partagez vos propres conseils et questions sur git !"

*Merci de noter que c'est un meetup pour celles ou ceux qui utilisent déjà un peu Git, commit, push et un peu les branches, et GitHub ou autre plate-forme. Ce n'est pas pour découvrir Git depuis 0, mais depuis une pratique basique.*

After kicking off our "From Zero to Hero in R" series with an introduction to R and its role in Data Science, it's time to get hands-on! In this second session, we’ll guide you step by step through setting up your R and RStudio environment so you can start coding with confidence.

title: Reproducible analysis pipelines with targets
description:
**Join Us for a Special Event with Olivia Angelin-Bonnet!**

We are thrilled to welcome ***Olivia Angelin-Bonnet***, a statistical scientist at The New Zealand Institute for Plant and Food Research Limited.
She is a real-world practitioner focusing on data analysis and a professional plot-maker. She is passionate about Systems Biology, ‘omics data analysis and integration, and the study of biological networks from a statistical and computational perspective.

**Topic:**

Data science projects typically consist of complex, multi-step analyses, which leads to the development of multiple interdependent scripts, often involving intensive computations, and requiring manual execution. Ensuring reproducibility is crucial, not only for transparency and collaboration, but also for adapting to changes in input data and stakeholder feedback. In this talk, ***Olivia*** will show how to use the targets R package to turn analysis scripts into reproducible pipelines. By automatically tracking dependencies between the analysis steps, caching intermediate results, and detecting changes in data or code, targets streamlines code execution and ensures consistency between inputs, computations, and outputs. Attendees will learn how to integrate targets into their projects to improve the efficiency, reproducibility, and maintainability of their code.

We look forward to seeing you online or in-person :)

title: Data Challenge 2025
description:
RLadies Ottawa invites you to our third annual data hackathon to celebrate International Women's day!

At our `posit::conf(2024)` watch party we watched [“GitHub: How To Tell Your Professional Story” by Abigail Haddad](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=R-bPjavai5A&list=PL9HYL-VRX0oSFkdF4fJeY63eGDvgofcbn&index=21). In this presentation Haddad highlighted the importance of building a compelling portfolio that not only highlights your skills but explains your professional identity.

For this year’s data challenge we encourage you to find YOUR passion project to round out your portfolio and develop your professional story! Take a look at your GitHub profile and ask yourself, *what type of project is missing here*?

For example, if you participated in [last year’s challenge](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2024/iwd.html#submissions) and created a traditional plot of some sort, this year try creating a visualization using a table, dashboard or another-outside-the-box presentation tool.

You can find the [full details](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2025/iwd.html) on our website.

For more inspo, be sure to check out our upcoming in-person event [Coding for a Cause](https://www.meetup.com/rladies-ottawa/events/306232274/?eventOrigin=group_upcoming_events) with Claudie Larouche on March 18th.

**Guidelines**

Although this challenge is hosted by *R-Ladies* Ottawa, you don’t have to use R to participate. You could use R, Python, a combination of the two, or anything you’re comfortable with!

No previous programming experience is required! If you’re interested in participating, but aren’t sure where to start, check out the recordings from our [Introduction to R workshops](https://rladies-ottawa.gitub.io/events/2024/intro_to_r.html) that we hosted in early 2024. The workshops will give you a solid foundation in data analysis and data visualization using R.

While anyone is welcome to participate in the challenge and submit an entry, **only submissions by women and gender minorities who reside in Canada will be eligible for the prizes.**

You can participate individually, or in teams of up to 5 people!

The most creative submission, chosen by the organizing team, will receive a $25 virtual gift card to [Maker House,](https://makerhouse.com/) generously donated by them. Maker House offers a curated collection of gifts and homewares, crafted locally in Ottawa and Canada 🍁

**How to submit an entry**

This will be a month-long data challenge, and you’ll have until **April 4th** to submit your entry. Your submission can be in any form you like – it can be a Quarto document, an image, a link to a webpage, or a link to a video, for example.

There are two options for submitting an entry:

* Option 1 (preferred option): If you have experience with GitHub and Quarto, you can create a pull request on [this repository](https://github.com/RLadies-Ottawa/rladies-ottawa.github.io) and add your submission to the [“Submissions”](https://rladies-ottawa.github.io/events/2024/iwd.html#submissions) part of [this page](https://github.com/RLadies-Ottawa/RLadies-Ottawa.github.io/blob/main/events/2025/iwd.qmd)
* Option 2: If you don’t have experience with GitHub, you can email [ottawa@rladies.org](mailto:ottawa@rladies.org) a link to your submission materials and we will add the link to our website.

After the submission deadline, R-Ladies Ottawa will host a virtual event where participants can showcase their work. This is a great way to learn from one another and to connect with other like-minded individuals!

The date and time of the showcase event will be announced soon.

title: PGEforge: Towards an open analysis ecosystem for Plasmodium genomic epidemiology
description:
**\*\*UPDATE: THIS EVENT HAS BEEN RESCHEDULED from 27th Mar to 03 Apr\*\***

Major advances in sequencing technologies, bioinformatics pipelines, and data analysis tools have transformed our ability to study malaria transmission and parasite evolution, providing valuable insights into malaria epidemiology from parasite genomic data. However, translating this genomic data into actionable insights for national malaria control programs remains a challenge. Many existing tools lack standardized workflows, comprehensive documentation, or interoperability, making it difficult for researchers and decision-makers to extract meaningful information.

To address this, Shazia and her team developed PGEforge (https://mrc-ide.github.io/PGEforge), a centralized, open-access repository for malaria genomic data analysis including analysis workflows, software evaluations, and reproducible tutorials. The Plasmodium Genomic Epidemiology (PlasmoGenEpi) network convened 18 experts to assess existing analysis tools, define key use cases for genomic data, and establish objective criteria for software evaluation. This initiative identified 40 Plasmodium genomic analysis tools with 22 prioritized for evaluation, defined eight key use cases for genomic data, and developed user-friendly reproducible tutorials for ten tools using shared datasets—all now accessible via PGEforge. Open-access platforms like PGEforge can empower researchers to adopt standardized practices and can help bridge the gap between genomic data generation, downstream analysis, and the effective use of these tools for malaria molecular surveillance towards actionable information for control programs.

In this talk, Shazia will showcase the functionalities of PGEforge, including real-world applications of genomic data use cases and analysis workflows, our approach to reproducible tutorials, and how we built this community-driven resource at a hackathon event using Quarto!

We look forward to seeing you there 💜

title: Ciencia de datos para la memoria: repensando la dictadura desde R
description:
A 49 años del inicio de la última dictadura militar en Argentina y en conmemoración de las víctimas del genocidio, proponemos nuevas miradas y métodos desde las Ciencias Sociales y el análisis de datos, que posibilitan abordajes novedosos. A través del uso de la herramienta R y la técnica de Web Scraping, es posible recuperar información y cruzar bases de datos para enriquecer nuestra comprensión del pasado, tanto desde una perspectiva sociológica como histórica.
En este evento, presentaremos un recorrido por los recursos disponibles, como el paquete "Presentes" de R. Además Manuel Bruccoleri Ochoa nos presentará los aportes realizados a “Presentes” y algunos resultados inéditos obtenidos.

description:
報名連結：[https://www.accupass.com/go/ai_bootcamp_taipei25](https://www.accupass.com/go/ai_bootcamp_taipei25)

**[Agenda]**

* 13:00 \~ 13:30 報到
* 13:30 \~ 14:00 Global AI Bootcamp Keynote
* 14:00 \~ 14:40 公開！微軟的 RAG 新招式- Ben
* 14:40 \~ 14:50 Break
* 14:50 \~ 15:30 AI agent 的基礎：Tool Call- Koko
* 15:30 \~ 15:40 Break
* 15:40 \~ 16:20 打造更聰明的 AI 代理人：從 0 到最佳化的實戰指南- Summit
* 16:20 \~ 16:30 Break
* 16:30 \~ 17:10 Interacting with Multimodal models in GitHub Models and Microsoft Copilot- Kristen
* 17:10 \~ 17:30 自由交流
* 17:30 活動結束

**[Talk Information]**

*📣 公開！微軟的 RAG 新招式*
微軟近來推出了一系列 RAG（檢索增強生成） 技術新解方，讓 AI 搜尋與知識應用更強大。本次分享將為你帶來一場 RAG 技術大補帖，拆解 Azure AI Search 最新功能、GraphRAG 的技術亮點，並帶你從 爬蟲、向量資料庫建立，一路實作到 Chatbot 應用。如果你對 AI 搜尋、RAG、Chatbot 應用 有興趣，這場分享將讓你一次掌握最新趨勢與實作技巧！

*🤖 AI agent 的基礎：Tool Call*
AI 代理人（Agent）能夠執行複雜任務的關鍵，在於如何正確地「呼叫工具」（Tool Call）。無論是檢索資訊、計算數值，還是與外部 API 互動，Tool Call 是 LLM 提升能力、接軌真實應用的基礎。本次分享將帶你了解 Tool Call 的核心概念，探討 LLM 如何決策與執行指令，並透過實作範例，讓你掌握讓 AI 代理人更聰明的第一步。適合對 AI 應用開發有興趣的開發者與技術愛好者！

*🔨 打造更聰明的 AI 代理人：從 0 到最佳化的實戰指南*
隨著大型語言模型（LLM）的發展，AI 代理人（Agent）已經成為許多應用的核心，但要讓它們真正發揮價值，從搭建到優化的過程至關重要。本次分享將帶你快速理解 AI 代理人的基礎架構，並探討如何提升其輸出品質，避免常見的陷阱與挑戰。無論你是剛入門還是希望進一步提升效能，這場演講都能幫助你建立更強大的 AI 代理人。

*💼 Interacting with Multimodal models in GitHub Models and Microsoft Copilot*
在這場實作體驗中，探索 Azure OpenAI 的 GPT-4o 多模態模型，並在 GitHub Models 和 Microsoft Copilot 上發揮創新應用。學習核心概念與最佳實踐，掌握如何透過 GPT-4o-mini 和 Copilot 生成 文本、聲音與圖像，有效提升 AI 互動與創作能力。

資料科學家，擅長以資料科學的視角切入，應對各類產業挑戰，涵蓋政府機關、非營利組織、產業數位轉型與生成式 AI 的實務應用。深信資料科學的價值在於實際落地，而非僅限於理論，致力於為組織創造具體效益。
現任瑞嘉軟體首席資料科學家，並以微軟 AI MVP 身份活躍於技術社群，同時擔任 R-Ladies Taipei 協辦人，推動資料科學的交流與教育。經常受邀擔任顧問，提供從數據分析到創新落地的全方位解決方案。無論是拆解問題、優化流程，還是探索新技術，始終專注於讓資料科學成為解決問題的強大工具。

title: Lunch&Learn: Quarto with brand.yml with Isabella Velásquez
description:
Maintaining a consistent brand identity across data science outputs is essential for clear communication and a professional image. This talk introduces brand.yml, a powerful new tool designed to streamline adherence to organizational style guides within the Quarto publishing system. brand.yml simplifies the creation of visually consistent materials that perfectly align with your organization's brand guidelines in HTML reports, presentations, websites, and dashboards.

We'll explore how brand.yml allows you to define your brand's visual identity, including colors, fonts, logos, and more, in a single, centralized location. You will learn how to integrate this configuration file into your Quarto projects, automatically applying the defined style guide to various outputs like reports, presentations, websites, and more. Beyond basic integration, I’ll share practical tips and demonstrate tools that simplify the process of designing and managing your brand.yml file to make brand customization even easier.

Join us to discover how brand.yml can elevate your Quarto documents to a new level of professionalism to ensure that your work is more impactful and on-brand.
